title: Teleste launches compact 1.8GHz amps tailored for Europe
summary: Teleste has launched a pair of new, compact 1.8GHz amps – the AC1800 and AC2800 – that are designed for street cabinets used by European cable operators. #pressrelease

title: Emma Falck named president of Nokia's mobile infrastructure unit
summary: Nokia has appointed Emma Falck as president of mobile infrastructure, effective September 1. She most recently served as EVP, products, smart infrastructure buildings at Siemens. #pressrelease

title: About 20% of Starlink subs are coming from cable – study
summary: Cable is contributing about 20% to Starlink's gross adds, a new study finds. Starlink is not yet having a material competitive impact on cable broadband, but it has grabbed the industry's attention.

title: Nextlink lights up first BEAD-funded FWA tower
summary: Nextlink said it has activated its first wireless tower to be funded by the BEAD program. The deployment is delivering FWA services to 104 locations in Bienville Parish, Louisiana. #pressrelease

title: Austria's Witke selects Vecima's virtual CMTS and DAA gear
summary: Witke, an Austrian broadband operator, is deploying a virtual CMTS and remote PHY devices from Vecima Networks for a DOCSIS network upgrade. #pressrelease

title: Inter Venezuela teams with Harmonic on PON-based 5G backhaul
summary: Inter Venezuela tapping into Harmonic's 'cOS' platform to support an XGS-PON-based mobile backhaul service. #pressrelease

title: Arelion adds 400G EVPL to bridge AI connectivity gap
summary: The service offers long-distance private connectivity for data center interconnection and large enterprise networks. #pressrelease

title: Orange Cyberdefense expands into Spain in collaboration with MasOrange
summary: Orange Cyberdefense teams will initially be based in Madrid and Barcelona, supported by a dedicated local Security Center (CyberSOC). #pressrelease

title: Taiwan Mobile net profit up 13% as it sets out tech roadmap
summary: Growth surge from new enterprise services and cost discipline drive a 13% jump in profit at Taiwan Mobile, as it seeks to increase focus on new growth areas.

title: New BEAD guidance offers 'clear and material benefit to Starlink' – analyst
summary: The NTIA's recently circulated guidance for the BEAD program seeks to restrict states from enforcing certain regulations in a move that could cause further confusion and delays and ultimately benefit Starlink, says Blair Levin.

title: DT to continue focus on AI and defense after steady Q1
summary: Deutsche Telekom is doubling down in its focus on defense and AI, as its CEO Tim Höttges discussed during a press conference unveiling a stable set of financial results for Q1.

title: HGC gains China ISP license via MIIT's pilot program
summary: HGC Global Communications has received approval from China's MIIT to join the pilot program that will allow the telecom company to operate a wholly owned ISP business in the country.

title: Eurobites: Telcos need to shape up to seize growth opportunity – Ericsson
summary: Also in today's EMEA regional roundup: Orange and friends plan new Europe-Africa link; Kyivstar has a perky Q1; Harion steps down at Tele2.

title: Greenwatch: Big Tech's carbon moves matter for telecom
summary: In a roundup of telecom sustainability news, we look at Big Tech's power moves, efforts to improve energy efficiency and BT's appetite for old plastics.

title: AT&T phaseout of old Nokia RAN boosted performance, says Ookla
summary: Data shared by Ookla shows AT&T's switch from Nokia to a 'single vendor' deal with Ericsson has delivered network improvements.

title: Nokia rolls out agentic AI for home and broadband networks
summary: Nokia has introduced new agentic AI capabilities for its fixed network product lines that aim to boost operational efficiencies and accelerate fiber rollouts. #pressrelease

title: Calix paves path to 50G-PON
summary: Calix is extending 50G-PON support to its Calix One platform for residential, business and MDU connectivity deployments. #pressrelease

title: FCC OKs EchoStar spectrum sales with $2.4B escrow that EchoStar didn't want
summary: The FCC's approval of EchoStar's spectrum sales to AT&T and SpaceX requires that EchoStar set up a $2.4 billion escrow account for claims from infrastructure companies that believe they are owed money from Dish Wireless.

title: Harmonic still reliant on Comcast and Charter, but customer base is diversifying
summary: Comcast and Charter represented more than half of Harmonic's broadband revenues in Q1 2026, but the vendor is seeing some growth in its 'rest of market' segment.

title: Lumen expands its US network with NorthLine, new northern fiber route
summary: Seattle to Minneapolis route will deliver 100G and 400G wavelength services to support large-scale traffic flows across a growing US corridor. #pressrelease
